HPI: 45 year old woman admitted to T/SICU from the OR following a    transsternal T4 vertebrectomy and mass removal. Pt. with alcoholic    cirrhosis and had 5L blood loss in OR. Pt was also recently discharged from [**Hospital 1711**]    Hospital ([**Date range (2) 6007**]?) for ETOH detoxification. T4 vertebrectomy via transsternal approach    Assessment:    Long OR course with large blood loss.  Arrived sedated on propofol and    requiring neo for BP maintenance. Two right sided chest tubes with    large sanginous output to water seal. Multiple areas of ecchymosis    especially on back and perineal area. Last HCT 35, INR 1.6.  Sternal/R    thoracotomy incision closed with dermabond, open to air. Intubated.    Lumbar drain in place without drainage. Lactate 2.9.    Action:    Q 1-2 hour neuro checks done, thoracic CT obtain, CXR X 2 done,    frequent lab draws, 2 units prbc, 2 units ffp, bag of platelets given.    Q 1 hour chest tube output recorded and patency ensured.  Lumbar drain    positioned in effort to obtain 15cc CSF output q 1 hour. Dr [**First Name (STitle) **]    assessed drain patency at 01:00.    Response:    Awakes to name while on low dose propofol, follows commands but gets    agitated and clamps on endotracheal tube. Follow commands readily with    arms, wiggles l toes and withdraws R leg to pain. CT output slowed,    continued to require low dose neo. Initially lumbar drain did not    drain, now with 12cc/hr when level at -5 cm below shoulder.    Plan:    Continue to monitor neuro status, ct output, labs.  Consider weaning to    extubation when stabilized.    Coagulopathy ..pt with alcohol cirrhosis    Assessment:    Large abdomen with ascities.  Hct 22, INR 1.6, platelets down to 80    multiple areas of ecchymosis and oozing from central lines. Large CT    output    Action:    PRBCs X 2 given, 2 units ffp, 1 bag of platelets. Frequent lab    monitoring.    Response:    Decrease in CT output, less oozing from central lines, stabilizing    hemodynamics    Plan:    Continue to monitor closely.    Pain control (acute pain, chronic pain)    Assessment:    Large thoracic/sternal incision. Initially on propofol only.  Was    previously on neurontin and Dilaudid for back pain.    Action:    Pain assessment q 2-4 hours, fentanyl iv as well as repositioning for    pain reduction    Response:    Pt. nods that pain is acceptable. Because of intubated status, unable    to use pain scale.    Plan:    Continue frequent assessment. Judicious use of narcotics as indicated    combined with positioning techniques. Restart adjunctive pain measures    today (lidocaine patch and neurontin). When extubated, consider    switching to PCA medication.
